Operation 'posttransactionevent' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. Added: (1) clarification of event types and their meanings, (2) input requirements (EVSE UID, protocol version), (3) output/response information, (4) side effects (asynchronous processing, state updates). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Submit a transaction event (Started, Ended, or Updated) for a specific EVSE.
data.description_suggestion: Submit a transaction event to report charging session state changes for a specific EVSE. Accepts transaction events with types: Started (charging session initiated), Ended (charging session completed), or Updated (session state changed). Requires EVSE UID and payload protocol version in path. Returns success confirmation. Events are processed asynchronously and update the charging station's transaction state in the system.
data.reasoning: Current description is too brief and lacks critical details for AI agent decision-making. Added: (1) clarification of event types and their meanings, (2) input requirements (EVSE UID, protocol version), (3) output/response information, (4) side effects (asynchronous processing, state updates). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation and what to expect.

message: Operation 'postmetervalues' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description lacks essential context for AI agents. Added: (1) what meter values contain (energy, power, measurements), (2) input requirements (EVSE UID, protocol version), (3) output/response information, (4) side effects (storage for billing/analytics/optimization), (5) rationale for update frequency recommendation. This enables AI agents to understand the operation's purpose in the charging workflow and when to invoke it.

data.current_description: Submit meter values for a specific EVSE. Recommended update frequency is 300 seconds or lower.
data.description_suggestion: Submit meter readings and sampled values from a charging station for a specific EVSE. Accepts meter value data including energy consumption, power, and other electrical measurements with timestamps. Requires EVSE UID and uses OCPP 2.0.1 protocol. Recommended submission frequency is 300 seconds or lower to maintain accurate charging session monitoring. Returns success confirmation. Meter values are stored for billing, analytics, and smart charging optimization.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks essential context for AI agents. Added: (1) what meter values contain (energy, power, measurements), (2) input requirements (EVSE UID, protocol version), (3) output/response information, (4) side effects (storage for billing/analytics/optimization), (5) rationale for update frequency recommendation. This enables AI agents to understand the operation's purpose in the charging workflow and when to invoke it.
